title: Project Metadata, Python Packaging, Entry Points, Plugins, and Clean Installs · Language Clinic: Resource Loading
summary: The generator runs only from its repository, imports templates through the current directory, has unbounded dependencies, discovers arbitrary plugins, omits package data, and is never installed into a clean environment. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource loading increment in installed reproducible static-site command. Learners progres…
objectives: Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.
Expose an import-safe console script with argument parsing, status codes, stdout and stderr contracts, help, version, and module execution parity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-packaging-cli-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_packaging_cli_3(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-resource-loading-1qex9x
# Competency: ssg-resource-loading.
# Case 6195: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical.
def evidence_ssg_resource_loading_1qex9x(artifacts: int = 2, inspected: int = 2, clean_installs: int = 3, command_smokes: int = 3, unsafe_plugins: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1qex9x = "1qex9x-x9xeq1-6195"
    assert evidence_variant_1qex9x
    evidence_axes_1qex9x = (
        "reader1qex9x",
        "source6195",
        "parsex9xeq1",
        "route1qex9x6195",
        "artifact61951qex9x",
        "recoveryx9xeq16195",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1qex9x)) == 6
    if artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-shape"
    if inspected != artifacts:
        return False, inspected, "artifact-inspection"
    if clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs:
        return False, command_smokes, "clean-install"
    if unsafe_plugins > 0:
        return False, unsafe_plugins, "plugin-boundary"
    assert inspected == artifacts
    return True, command_smokes, "installed-generator"

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Loading model · Theory Resource Loading, keep Resource Loading connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Loading model · Theory Resource Loading, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Resource Loading precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Loading model · Theory Resource Loading, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 7593 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to verify inspected package artifacts, clean command installs, resources, and plugin limits; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load packaged templates, schemas, and default assets through supported resource APIs rather than current working directory assumptions.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Resource Loading model · Theory Resource Loading, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: Relative paths beside source modules work after wheel installation.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-resource-loading-1qex9x.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-resource-loading-1qex9x(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_resource_loading_1qex9x\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?artifacts != 2 or inspected < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?inspected != artifacts)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?clean_installs < 2 or command_smokes != clean_installs)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unsafe_plugins > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
